requirements.text: WHEN the active project is favorited, the system shall display favorite state on the active project card.
WHEN the active project would otherwise fall outside the visible subset based on ordering rules, the system shall prioritize the active project to remain visible.
WHEN an inactive visible project is rendered, the system shall not display title or description expansion in the rail.
WHEN the panel opens, the system shall display the full project list as cards containing project code, title, and description.
WHEN a project in the panel is favorited, the system shall display favorite state on the full project card.
WHEN the panel renders, the system shall order favorites first, then remaining projects.
WHEN ordering remaining non-favorite projects in the panel, the system shall sort by lastUsedAt descending, with count descending as tie-break.
WHEN favorites do not fit in the visible rail, the system shall display those favorites at the top of the expanded project list.
WHEN a user selects a project from the selector rail, the system shall change the current project to the selected project.
WHEN a user selects a project from the full project panel, the system shall change the current project to the selected project.
WHEN project selection is committed, the system shall update usage state for the selected project.
WHEN usage state is updated, the system shall set lastUsedAt to the current timestamp.
WHEN usage state is updated, the system shall increment count.
WHEN the selector rail renders, the system shall display the active project first.
WHEN filling remaining visible rail slots, the system shall prioritize favorites first, then non-favorites.
WHEN ordering visible favorites, the system shall sort by count descending, with lastUsedAt descending as tie-break.
WHEN ordering visible non-favorites, the system shall sort by lastUsedAt descending, with count descending as tie-break.
WHEN the active project would otherwise fall outside the visible subset based on ordering rules, the system shall prioritize the active project to remain visible regardless of favorite status.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/user.toml contains [ui.projectSelector].visibleCount, the system shall use that value as the number of visible projects in the rail.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/user.toml contains [ui.projectSelector].compactInactive, the system shall use that value to control inactive visible project presentation.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/user.toml is missing, the system shall fall back to default values without breaking the UI.
WHEN visibleCount in user.toml is invalid, the system shall fall back to the default value (7).
WHEN compactInactive in user.toml is invalid, the system shall fall back to the default value (true).
WHEN mutable selector state changes, the system shall persist the state to CONFIG_DIR/project-selector.json.
WHEN persisting selector state, the system shall key entries by project code.
WHEN persisting selector state, the system shall store favorite, lastUsedAt, and count per project.
WHEN persisting after project selection, the system shall write shortly after the selection changes and shall not depend on shutdown behavior.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/project-selector.json is missing, the system shall initialize empty state without breaking the UI.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/project-selector.json is invalid JSON, the system shall fall back to empty state without breaking the UI.
WHEN CONFIG_DIR/project-selector.json contains partially malformed entries, the system shall load valid project entries and drop invalid entries.
WHEN responsive presentation adapts by viewport, the system shall preserve the same selector state model and expanded project list behavior.
WHEN visibleCount is not an integer greater than or equal to 1, the system shall fall back to the default value (7).
WHEN compactInactive is not a boolean, the system shall fall back to the default value (true).
WHEN a project-state entry's favorite is not a boolean, the system shall drop that field or entry.
WHEN a project-state entry's lastUsedAt is present but not a valid ISO-8601 timestamp, the system shall drop that field.
WHEN a project-state entry's count is not an integer greater than or equal to 0, the system shall treat it as 0.
WHEN unknown fields appear in user.toml or project-selector.json, the system shall ignore them safely.
WHEN a per-project state entry is invalid, the system shall drop that entry without preventing other valid entries from loading.
User preferences stored in CONFIG_DIR/user.toml under [ui.projectSelector]
Mutable selector state stored in CONFIG_DIR/project-selector.json, keyed by project code
Usage state writes occur shortly after project selection, not on shutdown
Full project browser panel is visually attached to the selector
visibleCount default is 7; compactInactive default is true
Mutable selector state remains separate from immutable user preferences
Active project always counts toward visibleCount but is never hidden
No All Projects synthetic item is introduced
WHEN the user clicks the favorite star on any project card, the system shall toggle the favorite state and persist the change.
WHEN the selector rail renders, the system shall display the active project as a larger card containing project code, title, and description.
WHEN the user clicks the active project card, the system shall open the full project browser panel.
WHEN the user hovers over an inactive project chip, the system shall display a hover card with full project details after a short delay.
WHEN a hover card displays, the system shall show the project code, name, description, and favorite status.
WHEN the user stops hovering over an inactive project chip, the system shall dismiss the hover card after a short delay.
WHEN compactInactive is true (default), the system shall render inactive visible rail projects as compact code-only cards.
WHEN the viewport is mobile-sized, the system shall display only the active project in the collapsed selector rail.

scenarios.title: Favorite indicator on active project card
scenarios.given: I am viewing the application and the active project is favorited
scenarios.then: I see the favorite state displayed on the active project card

scenarios.title: Active project always visible regardless of ordering
scenarios.given: the active project would fall outside the visible subset based on normal ordering rules
scenarios.then: the active project is prioritized and remains visible in the rail

scenarios.title: Select project from selector rail changes current project
scenarios.given: I am viewing the selector rail with multiple visible projects
scenarios.when: I select an inactive project from the rail
scenarios.then: the selected project becomes the current project and its usage state is updated with incremented count and current timestamp

scenarios.title: Select project from panel changes current project
scenarios.given: I have opened the full project panel
scenarios.when: I select a project from the panel
scenarios.then: the selected project becomes the current project and its usage state is updated with incremented count and current timestamp

scenarios.title: Rail ordering prioritizes favorites then sorts by usage
scenarios.given: I have multiple projects with various favorite states and usage patterns
scenarios.then: the active project appears first, followed by favorites sorted by count descending, then non-favorites sorted by lastUsedAt descending

scenarios.title: Configuration controls visible count and compact mode
scenarios.given: the user configuration file may have custom visibleCount and compactInactive settings
scenarios.when: the selector renders with valid configuration values
scenarios.then: the rail respects the configured visibleCount and compactInactive; invalid or missing values fall back to defaults (visibleCount=7, compactInactive=true)

scenarios.title: State persists to file after project selection
scenarios.given: I select a project and the selector state changes
scenarios.then: the state is written to project-selector.json keyed by project code containing favorite, lastUsedAt, and count; missing or invalid files are handled gracefully without breaking the UI

scenarios.title: Panel displays full project list with favorites first
scenarios.given: I have opened the full project panel
scenarios.then: I see all projects as cards with code, title, and description; favorites appear first with favorite indicators, followed by non-favorites sorted by lastUsedAt descending

scenarios.title: Toggle favorite by clicking star
scenarios.given: I am viewing the project selector with favorited and unfavorited projects
scenarios.when: I click the star icon on any project card
scenarios.then: the favorite state toggles for that project and the change is persisted to project-selector.json

scenarios.title: Active project shown as larger card with code, title, and description
scenarios.given: I am viewing the application with an active project selected
scenarios.then: I see the active project displayed as a larger card containing the project code, title, and description

scenarios.title: Inactive visible projects display based on compact mode setting
scenarios.given: I have inactive visible projects in the selector rail

scenarios.title: Mobile viewport shows collapsed selector rail
scenarios.given: I am viewing the application on a mobile-sized viewport
scenarios.then: I see only the active project; clicking the active project provides access to remaining projects with the same state model

scenarios.title: Active project card click opens full project browser
scenarios.given: I am viewing the application with an active project selected
scenarios.when: I click the active project card
scenarios.then: the full project browser panel opens displaying all projects

scenarios.title: Hover card displays on inactive project chip hover
scenarios.given: I am viewing the selector rail with inactive project chips visible
scenarios.when: I hover over an inactive project chip
scenarios.then: a hover card appears after a short delay showing the project code, name, description, and favorite status; when I stop hovering, the card dismisses after a short delay

obligations.title: Load selector data from single /api/config/selector endpoint combining user.toml preferences and project-selector.json state
Prioritize active project to remain visible in rail even when it would fall outside normal visible subset based on ordering rules
Panel displays all registered projects as cards with code, title, description; favorites first (by count desc, lastUsedAt desc), then non-favorites (by lastUsedAt desc, count desc)
Project selection from rail or panel changes current project via useProjectManager.setSelectedProject and updates usage state (lastUsedAt timestamp, increment count)
Display active project first, then favorites (by count desc, lastUsedAt desc), then non-favorites (by lastUsedAt desc, count desc) within visibleCount limit
Load visibleCount and compactInactive from user.toml [ui.projectSelector]; fall back to defaults (visibleCount=7, compactInactive=true) when missing or invalid
Persist selector state (favorite, lastUsedAt, count per project) to project-selector.json keyed by project code; write shortly after selection changes; handle missing or invalid JSON gracefully
Validate config values: visibleCount >=1 integer, compactInactive boolean; drop invalid project-state entries or fields; ignore unknown fields
Render inactive visible rail projects as compact chips
Display hover card with full project details on inactive chip hover
Active project card click opens full project browser panel
On mobile viewport, display only active project card; clicking it provides access to remaining projects
Display active project as larger card containing code and title with favorite indicator; clicking opens browser panel
E2E tests for selector behavior; Jest unit tests for ordering and data hook logic

artifacts.sourceRef: Pure ordering functions for rail and panel
Mutable selector state keyed by project code
Jest unit tests for ordering functions

tasks.title: Create missing infrastructure files and verify dependencies
Implement useProjectSelectorManager hook for visible subset computation
Create ProjectBrowserPanel component with full project list
Implement useSelectorData hook with configuration loading and persistence
Integrate project switching with useProjectManager and state persistence
Create ProjectSelectorCard component for active project display
Create ProjectSelector index component with panel state management
Create ProjectSelectorChip component for inactive project display
Create ProjectSelectorRail component with responsive layout
